---
title: 'Bayesian Logistic Regression'
author: "DS 4420 Final Project"
output:
  pdf_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

## Load Libraries

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(brms)
```

## Load and Prepare Data

```{r}
df <- read.csv("../data/boston_crime_combined.csv")

# clean up SHOOTING column
df$SHOOTING <- ifelse(df$SHOOTING %in% c(1, "Y"), 1, 0)
df$SHOOTING[is.na(df$SHOOTING)] <- 0

# keep only relevant columns
df <- df[, c("SHOOTING", "MONTH", "HOUR", "DAY_OF_WEEK", "DISTRICT")]

# data is already cleaned in data_prep, just double check
df <- df[complete.cases(df), ]

# encode DAY_OF_WEEK as numeric
day_levels <- c("Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday")
df$DAY_OF_WEEK <- as.numeric(factor(df$DAY_OF_WEEK, levels = day_levels))

# brms needs SHOOTING as factor for bernoulli family
df$SHOOTING <- as.factor(df$SHOOTING)

cat("Size after cleaning:", nrow(df), "\n")
cat("Any NAs:", anyNA(df), "\n")
cat("Shooting distribution:\n")
print(table(df$SHOOTING))
```

## Undersample to Balance Classes

```{r}
set.seed(42)

shooting_yes <- df[df$SHOOTING == 1, ]
shooting_no  <- df[df$SHOOTING == 0, ]

# randomly sample same number of non-shooting as shooting
shooting_no_sampled <- shooting_no[sample(nrow(shooting_no), nrow(shooting_yes)), ]

df_balanced <- rbind(shooting_yes, shooting_no_sampled)
df_balanced <- df_balanced[sample(nrow(df_balanced)), ]

cat("Balanced dataset size:", nrow(df_balanced), "\n")
cat("Shooting distribution after undersampling:\n")
print(table(df_balanced$SHOOTING))
```

## Train / Test Split

```{r}
set.seed(42)

n <- nrow(df_balanced)
train_idx <- sample(1:n, size = floor(0.8 * n))

df_train <- df_balanced[train_idx, ]
df_test  <- df_balanced[-train_idx, ]

cat("Train size:", nrow(df_train), "\n")
cat("Test size:", nrow(df_test), "\n")
```

## Bayesian Logistic Regression via brms

```{r}
# check default priors for our model
def_prior <- default_prior(
  SHOOTING ~ MONTH + HOUR + DAY_OF_WEEK + DISTRICT,
  data   = df_train,
  family = bernoulli("logit")
)

print(def_prior)
```

```{r}
# fit the model
set.seed(42)
brm_fit <- brm(
  SHOOTING ~ MONTH + HOUR + DAY_OF_WEEK + DISTRICT,
  family  = bernoulli("logit"),
  data    = df_train,
  prior   = def_prior,
  chains  = 4,
  iter    = 2000,
  warmup  = 500,
  cores   = 1,
  seed    = 42
)
```

## Model Summary

```{r}
summary(brm_fit)
```

## Trace Plots

```{r}
# trace plots to check convergence
plot(brm_fit, ask = FALSE)
```

## Posterior Summaries

```{r}
# extract posterior draws
post_draws <- as.data.frame(brm_fit)

# get coefficient names
coef_cols <- grep("^b_", names(post_draws), value = TRUE)

# posterior mean and 95% credible intervals
post_mean <- colMeans(post_draws[, coef_cols])
post_ci   <- apply(post_draws[, coef_cols], 2, quantile, probs = c(0.025, 0.975))

summary_df <- data.frame(
  coefficient = coef_cols,
  mean        = round(post_mean, 4),
  lower_95    = round(post_ci[1, ], 4),
  upper_95    = round(post_ci[2, ], 4)
)

# flag coefficients whose CI doesn't include 0
summary_df$significant <- ifelse(
  summary_df$lower_95 > 0 | summary_df$upper_95 < 0, "*", ""
)

print(summary_df)
```

## Posterior Coefficient Plot

```{r}
ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = mean, y = reorder(coefficient, mean))) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", size = 2) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower_95, xmax = upper_95), height = 0.3, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Posterior Coefficients with 95% Credible Intervals",
       x = "Coefficient Value", y = "") +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Classification on Test Set

```{r}
# get posterior predictive probabilities on test set
prob_test <- posterior_epred(brm_fit, newdata = df_test)

# use posterior mean probability for classification
prob_mean <- colMeans(prob_test)
pred_test <- ifelse(prob_mean > 0.5, 1, 0)
y_test    <- as.numeric(as.character(df_test$SHOOTING))

conf_mat <- table(Predicted = pred_test, Actual = y_test)
print(conf_mat)

accuracy  <- mean(pred_test == y_test)
cat("Test accuracy:", round(accuracy, 4), "\n")

tp <- conf_mat[2, 2]
fp <- conf_mat[2, 1]
fn <- conf_mat[1, 2]

precision <- tp / (tp + fp)
recall    <- tp / (tp + fn)
f1        <- 2 * precision * recall / (precision + recall)

cat("Precision:", round(precision, 4), "\n")
cat("Recall:",    round(recall, 4), "\n")
cat("F1 Score:",  round(f1, 4), "\n")
```

## Posterior Predictive Check

```{r}
# distribution of test accuracy across posterior samples
n_check  <- 200
ppc_acc  <- numeric(n_check)

for (i in 1:n_check) {
  preds      <- ifelse(prob_test[i, ] > 0.5, 1, 0)
  ppc_acc[i] <- mean(preds == y_test)
}

ggplot(data.frame(accuracy = ppc_acc), aes(x = accuracy)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = accuracy, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(title = "Posterior Predictive Check: Distribution of Test Accuracy",
       subtitle = "Red line = accuracy using posterior mean",
       x = "Accuracy", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```
